summary: I read both design guides, then audited the iteration-2 Dashboard UI design (`design-writer/output/design.md`) against them, verifying each of iteration 1's five findings and scanning for new deviations. All five are genuinely resolved: F1's blind `rescue StandardError` is now licensed by a proposed backend-guide amendment (§4) per CLAUDE.md's rules; F2's query reads now execute in the controller inside the `safely` boundary and hand materialized data (a `Data` struct) to views that only read plain attributes; F3's activity broadcasts are audited across all eight call sites so the three transaction-wrapped ones fire after commit; F4's error banner moved from reserved amber to the semantic red soft-badge token; and F5's off-scale `mt-1` became `mt-2`. Broader guide checks (thin controllers, services/queries as the primary test unit, no business-logic callbacks, Tailwind type scale and semantic status colors, dom_id-targeted per-user Turbo streams rendering true state on load) also pass. The only residual — a whole-panel replace for the activity feed — is a self-declared, well-justified exception whose rationale is already covered by the proposed §8 amendment, so I logged it as a non-blocking observation. **Verdict: pass**, no findings, written to `guide-alignment-critic/verdict.json`.
